Easter (transcription)[[16]](#cite_note-16) | Christmas (transcription)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
Buona Pasqua a voi, uomini e donne di Roma e d’Italia! | Buon Natale agli abitanti di Roma e dell’intera Italia!

Le Christ est ressuscité. Sainte fête de Pâques ! Que pour vous ce mystère soit source de bonheur et de paix profonde. | Heureuse et sainte fête de Noël ! Que le Christ Sauveur vous garde dans l’espérance et qu’il vous fasse le don de la paix profonde !

May the grace and joy of the Risen Christ be with you all. | May the birth of the Prince of Peace remind the world where its true happiness lies; and may your hearts be filled with hope and joy, for the Saviour has been born for us.

Euch allen ein gesegnetes und frohes Osterfest! Der Friede und die Freude des auferstandenen Herrn sei mit Euch. | Die Geburt Jesu Christi, des Erlösers der Menschen, erfülle Euer Leben mit tiefer Freude und reicher Gnade; sein Friede möge in Euren Herzen wohnen. Gesegnete und frohe Weihnachten!

Os deseo a todos una buena y feliz fiesta de Pascua, con la paz y la alegría, la esperanza y el amor de Jesucristo Resucitado. | ¡Feliz Navidad! Que la Paz de Cristo reine en vuestros corazones, en las familias y en todos los pueblos.
Uma Páscoa feliz com Cristo Ressuscitado. | Feliz Natal para todos! O nascimento do Menino Jesus ilumine de alegria e paz vossos lares e Nações!
Zalig Pasen! Ik wil mijn hartelijke dank tot uitdrukking brengen voor de fraaie bloemen uit Nederland voor de Paasmis op het Sint Pietersplein. | Zalig en gelukkig Kerstmis.
Frou a geseent Oushteren. | Schéin Chreschtdag.
Χριστός άνέστη (Christós ánésti̱) | Καλά Χριστούγεννα (Kalá Christoúgenna)
Për shumë vjet Pashkët. | Per shum vjet Krishtlindjen.
Cristos a Înviat. | Sărbători Fericite de Crăciun si Annul Nou.
Krisztus feltámadott. Alleluja. | Áldott Karácsonyt.
Chrystus zmartwychwstał. | Błogosławionych świąt Bożego Narodzenia.
Chrystus z mortwych stanył. | Zohnowane hody! A zbožowne Nowe lěto!
Kristus vstal z mrtvých. | Narodil se vám Spasitel. Radujte se!
Radostné veľkonočné sviatky. | Milostiplné a radostné Vianočné Sviatky.
Blagoslovljene velikonočne praznike. | Božje Dete naj vam podeli svoj blagoslov.
Sretan Uskrs! | Sretan Božić, Isusovo Porođenje!
Христос васкрсе! (Hristos vaskrse!) | Среħан Божиħ – Христос се роди! (Srećan Božić – Hristos se rodi!)
Христос Воскресна. (Hristos Voskresna.) | Нека ви е честит Божиќ и Нова Година (Neka vi e čestit Božiḱ i Nova Godina)
Христос възкресе (Khristos vŭzkrese) | Честито Рождество Христово (Chestito Rozhdestvo Khristovo)
Христос Воскресе. (Hristos Voskrese.) | Сердечно поздравляю всех с Праздником Рождества Христова (Serdechno pozdravlyayu vsekh s Prazdnikom Rozhdestva Khristova)
Christos uvaskrós. | Viasiòłych kalàdnych Sviàtaû!
Христос Воскрес! (Khrystos Voskres!) | Веселих Свят з Різдвом Христовим і Новим Роком! (Veselykh Svyat z Rizdvom Khrystovym i Novym Rokom!)
ХРИСТИЙН ДАХИН АМИЛАЛЫН БАЯРЫН МЗНД ХУРГЗЕ! Khristiin Dakhin Amilalyn Bayaryn Mendkhurgeye! | Hristijn Tersen Edrijn mendhurgie

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Papal address and apostolic blessing given on certain special occasions

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8b/2008_Xmas_Urbi_Orbi_Pope_Benedict_XVI.jpg/250px-2008_Xmas_Urbi_Orbi_Pope_Benedict_XVI.jpg)](/wiki/File:2008_Xmas_Urbi_Orbi_Pope_Benedict_XVI.jpg)

The 2008 *Urbi et Orbi* given by [Pope Benedict XVI](/wiki/Pope_Benedict_XVI "Pope Benedict XVI") on [Christmas Day](/wiki/Christmas_Day "Christmas Day"), from [Saint Peter's Square](/wiki/Saint_Peter%27s_Square "Saint Peter's Square"), [Vatican City](/wiki/Vatican_City "Vatican City")

***Urbi et Orbi*** (Latin: [[ur.bi ˈet ˈor.bi]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Latin "Help:IPA/Latin"); [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'to the city [of [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome")] and to the world') denotes a papal address and [apostolic blessing](/wiki/Apostolic_blessing "Apostolic blessing") given by the [pope](/wiki/Pope "Pope") on certain solemn occasions.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The term *Urbi et Orbi* evolved from the consciousness of the ancient [Roman Empire](/wiki/Roman_Empire "Roman Empire"). The invocation is expressed by the pope in his capacity as both the bishop of Rome (*urbs* = city; *urbi* the corresponding [dative form](/wiki/Dative_case "Dative case"); compare: *urban*) and the head of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") throughout the world (*orbis* = earth; *orbi* the corresponding dative form; compare: *orbit*).[[1]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-1)

The formula is found more frequently in the language of the Church, as in the inscription at the [Lateran Basilica](/wiki/Archbasilica_of_Saint_John_Lateran "Archbasilica of Saint John Lateran"), which is: *Sacrosancta Lateranensis ecclesia omnium urbis et orbis ecclesiarum mater et caput* ("Most Holy Lateran Church, of all the churches in the city and the world, the mother and head").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In the 4th century, [Pope Damasus I](/wiki/Pope_Damasus_I "Pope Damasus I") wrote in a letter to the [bishops of Illyricum](/wiki/Diocese_of_Illyricum "Diocese of Illyricum"): *Unde iustum est, omnes in Universo Romanorum Orbe Doctores legis, ea, quae legis sunt, sapere, et non fidem doctrinis variis maculare* ("Wherefore it is just that all the teachers of the law in the universe of the Roman world should be wise in the things of the law, and not to tarnish the faith with various doctrines").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The ritual of the papal blessing *Urbi et Orbi* developed in the 13th century during the reign of [Pope Gregory X](/wiki/Pope_Gregory_X "Pope Gregory X").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Occasions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Occasions")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/Petersdom_%28Fassade%29.jpg/250px-Petersdom_%28Fassade%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Petersdom_(Fassade).jpg)

The façade of Saint Peter's Basilica with loggia balcony, where the pope usually gives the blessing *Urbi et Orbi*

The *Urbi et Orbi* address and blessing is the most solemn form of blessing in the Catholic Church, and is reserved for the most solemn occasions. These occasions include [Easter](/wiki/Easter "Easter"), [Christmas](/wiki/Christmas "Christmas"), and the proclamation of a newly elected pope concluding a conclave.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

*Urbi et Orbi* blessings are usually given from the central [loggia](/wiki/Loggia "Loggia") of [Saint Peter's Basilica](/wiki/Saint_Peter%27s_Basilica "Saint Peter's Basilica") in Vatican City, at noontime, and are broadcast worldwide through the [European Broadcasting Union](/wiki/European_Broadcasting_Union "European Broadcasting Union") and other linkups. The address concludes with greetings in many languages in relation to the feast celebrated.

The [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") grants a [plenary indulgence](/wiki/Plenary_indulgence "Plenary indulgence") by the willful grace and intent of the pope, on the usual conditions, to those who "devoutly receive" the blessing that the pope imparts *Urbi et Orbi*.

For any ordinary plenary indulgence, the conditions are:

* Reception of [sacramental confession](/wiki/Sacrament_of_Penance_(Catholic_Church) "Sacrament of Penance (Catholic Church)") through a [Catholic priest](/wiki/Catholic_priest "Catholic priest") within 20 days (before or after) of performing the specific work
* Reception of [Eucharistic communion](/wiki/Eucharist "Eucharist") within 20 days (before or after) of performing the specific work
* Prayers for the [intentions of the pope](/wiki/Holy_actions#Intentions "Holy actions") designated for that particular month or occasion, usually at the same time the work is performed, though recitation some days before or after also suffices

Gaining a plenary indulgence requires that a baptized Catholic must also exclude any attachment to [sin](/wiki/Sin "Sin"), even [venial sin](/wiki/Venial_sin "Venial sin").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Since 1985, this indulgence is granted not only to the people in [Saint Peter's Square](/wiki/Saint_Peter%27s_Square "Saint Peter's Square"), but also to those who though unable to be physically present, "piously follow" it by radio or television.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

This is now extended to all who receive the [papal blessing](/wiki/Papal_blessing "Papal blessing") over the Internet ("the new communications medium"), since the blessing is preceded by an announcement by a [cardinal](/wiki/Cardinal_(Catholic_Church) "Cardinal (Catholic Church)") (usually the [cardinal protodeacon](/wiki/Cardinal_protodeacon "Cardinal protodeacon")): "His Holiness Pope N. grants a plenary indulgence in the form laid down by the Church to all the faithful present and to those who receive his blessing by radio, television, and the new communications media. Let us ask Almighty God to grant the Pope many years as leader of the Church and peace and unity to the Church throughout the world."[[10]](#cite_note-pccs-10)

On 27 March 2020, Pope Francis imparted an extraordinary *Urbi et Orbi* blessing in order to pray for the end of the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[11]](#cite_note-11) He stood in a deserted [Saint Peter's Square](/wiki/Saint_Peter%27s_Square "Saint Peter's Square").[[12]](#cite_note-12) For this blessing, the [Apostolic Penitentiary](/wiki/Apostolic_Penitentiary "Apostolic Penitentiary") loosened the requirements to receive the [Eucharist](/wiki/Eucharist "Eucharist") and go to confession, due to the impossibility for people affected by lockdowns and suspension of liturgies.[[13]](#cite_note-13) The *[Salus Populi Romani](/wiki/Salus_Populi_Romani "Salus Populi Romani")* image and a crucifix from the church of [San Marcello al Corso](/wiki/San_Marcello_al_Corso "San Marcello al Corso")—both seen as miraculous—were brought to the square for the occasion. The Pope performed the blessing as a [Benediction of the Blessed Sacrament](/wiki/Benediction_of_the_Blessed_Sacrament "Benediction of the Blessed Sacrament").[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Greetings in different languages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Greetings in different languages")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Urbi_et_Orbi "Special:EditPage/Urbi et Orbi") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *(July 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

From [Pope Paul VI](/wiki/Pope_Paul_VI "Pope Paul VI") to [Pope Benedict XVI](/wiki/Pope_Benedict_XVI "Pope Benedict XVI"), after delivering their *Urbi et Orbi* message, the pope would greet the different nations in their native languages. The pope typically began by saying: "To those who listen to me, I address a cordial greeting in the different language expressions." [Pope Francis](/wiki/Pope_Francis "Pope Francis") discontinued this practice, but in turn was revived by [Pope Leo XIV](/wiki/Pope_Leo_XIV "Pope Leo XIV") beginning in 2025.

| Language | Easter (transcription)[[16]](#cite_note-16) | Christmas (transcription)[[17]](#cite_note-17) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Italian | Buona Pasqua a voi, uomini e donne di Roma e d’Italia! | Buon Natale agli abitanti di Roma e dell’intera Italia! |
| French | Le Christ est ressuscité. Sainte fête de Pâques ! Que pour vous ce mystère soit source de bonheur et de paix profonde. | Heureuse et sainte fête de Noël ! Que le Christ Sauveur vous garde dans l’espérance et qu’il vous fasse le don de la paix profonde ! |
| English | May the grace and joy of the Risen Christ be with you all. | May the birth of the Prince of Peace remind the world where its true happiness lies; and may your hearts be filled with hope and joy, for the Saviour has been born for us. |
| German | Euch allen ein gesegnetes und frohes Osterfest! ## Formulae of apostolic blessing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Formulae of apostolic blessing")]

### Latin

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Latin")]

:   *Sancti Apostoli Petrus et Paulus: de quorum potestate et auctoritate confidimus, ipsi intercedant pro nobis ad Dominum.*
:   *[℟](/wiki/Response_(liturgy) "Response (liturgy)")*: Amen.

:   *Precibus et meritis beatæ Mariæ semper Virginis, beati Michaelis Archangeli, beati Ioannis Baptistæ et sanctorum Apostolorum Petri et Pauli et omnium Sanctorum, misereatur vestri omnipotens Deus; et dimissis omnibus peccatis vestris, perducat vos Iesus Christus ad vitam æternam.*
:   *℟*: Amen.

:   *Indulgentiam, absolutionem, et remissionem omnium peccatorum vestrorum, spatium veræ et fructuosæ pœnitentiæ, cor semper pœnitens, et emendationem vitæ, gratiam et consolationem Sancti Spiritus; et finalem perseverantiam in bonis operibus tribuat vobis omnipotens et misericors Dominus.*
:   *℟*: Amen.

:   *Et benedictio Dei omnipotentis, Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti, descendat super vos et maneat semper.*
:   *℟*: Amen.[[10]](#cite_note-pccs-10)

### English translation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: English translation")]

:   May the Holy Apostles [Peter](/wiki/Saint_Peter "Saint Peter") and [Paul](/wiki/Paul_the_Apostle "Paul the Apostle"), in whose power and authority we trust, intercede for us before the Lord.
:   *℟*: Amen.
:   Through the prayers and merits of Blessed [Mary ever Virgin](/wiki/Mary_(mother_of_Jesus) "Mary (mother of Jesus)"), Saint [Michael the Archangel](/wiki/Michael_(archangel) "Michael (archangel)"), Saint [John the Baptist](/wiki/John_the_Baptist "John the Baptist"), the holy apostles [Peter and Paul](/wiki/Feast_of_Saints_Peter_and_Paul "Feast of Saints Peter and Paul"), and all the saints, may Almighty God have mercy on you and forgive all your sins, and may [Jesus Christ](/wiki/Jesus_Christ "Jesus Christ") bring you to everlasting life.
:   *℟:* Amen.
:   May the almighty and merciful Lord grant you indulgence, absolution and the remission of all your sins, a season of true and fruitful penance, a well-disposed heart, amendment of life, the grace and comfort of the [Holy Spirit](/wiki/Holy_Spirit "Holy Spirit") and final perseverance in good works.
:   *℟:* Amen.
:   And may the blessing of Almighty God, the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Spirit, come down on you and remain with you forever.
:   *℟:* Amen.[[10]](#cite_note-pccs-10)

## Former practice

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Former practice")]

Prior to the occupation of Rome by the army of the [Kingdom of Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy_(1861%E2%80%931946) "Kingdom of Italy (1861–1946)") (20 September 1870), this blessing was given more frequently and at specific [basilicas](/wiki/Basilica "Basilica") at Rome:

* [Saint Peter's Basilica](/wiki/Saint_Peter%27s_Basilica "Saint Peter's Basilica")—[Holy Thursday](/wiki/Holy_Thursday "Holy Thursday"), [Easter](/wiki/Easter "Easter"), the [Feast of Saints Peter and Paul](/wiki/Feast_of_Saints_Peter_and_Paul "Feast of Saints Peter and Paul"), and at the [papal coronation](/wiki/Papal_coronation "Papal coronation")
* [Archbasilica of St. John Lateran](/wiki/Archbasilica_of_St._John_Lateran "Archbasilica of St. John Lateran")—[Ascension](/wiki/Ascension_of_Jesus "Ascension of Jesus") (sometimes this was postponed until [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost")), and when a new pope was enthroned as [Bishop of Rome](/wiki/Bishop_of_Rome "Bishop of Rome")
* [Basilica of Saint Mary Major](/wiki/Basilica_of_Saint_Mary_Major "Basilica of Saint Mary Major")—[Assumption](/wiki/Assumption_of_Mary "Assumption of Mary")

On the occasion of a [Holy Year](/wiki/Holy_Year "Holy Year") the pope gave the blessing on other occasions too for the benefit of pilgrims. In the jubilee year of 1650 [Pope Innocent X](/wiki/Pope_Innocent_X "Pope Innocent X") did so at [Epiphany](/wiki/Epiphany_(holiday) "Epiphany (holiday)"), Pentecost, and [All Saints](/wiki/All_Saints%27_Day "All Saints' Day"). He and later popes gave such special-occasion blessings from the balcony of the [Quirinal Palace](/wiki/Quirinal_Palace "Quirinal Palace"), which was then the papal residence.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

[Pope Pius IX](/wiki/Pope_Pius_IX "Pope Pius IX"), who considered himself a "[prisoner in the Vatican](/wiki/Prisoner_in_the_Vatican "Prisoner in the Vatican")" gave the solemn blessing of *Urbi et Orbi* from the balcony of the [Quirinal Palace](/wiki/Quirinal_Palace "Quirinal Palace").[[1]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-1)

## Other uses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Urbi_et_Orbi&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other uses")]

It is the motto of [Long Island University](/wiki/Long_Island_University "Long Island University").[[19]](#cite_note-19)
